card_description: Jack Scovil is a historical figure referenced in Philip K. Dick's Exegesis. Appears in 2 Exegesis passages. Context: "After a business letter regarding a lost $500 check, Dick pivots to a theological breakthrough: the Kingdom of God is located in the "Future," and he reached it by "running out of time" in March 1974."

linked_segments.summary: After a business letter regarding a lost $500 check, Dick pivots to a theological breakthrough: the Kingdom of God is located in the "Future," and he reached it by "running out of time" in March 1974.
linked_segments.title: 1975-09-15_SECTION_012_01.txt

linked_segments.summary: Dick rejects the common Christian emphasis on suffering, arguing that Christ's purpose was to alter the ontology of the world. He discovers a "steganographic" message—the word **"Felix"**—embedded in
